title: Raina Kestrel
summary: A gutter-born scout who keeps finding ritual traces before anyone else notices them.
concept: Streetwise clue hound pulled into the chapel fallout before the rest of the city realizes how bad it is.

background.history: Raina learned the city by running messages for dock crews and market brokers until suspicious errands started leaving ritual residue behind.
background.incitingIncident: She found chapel wax sealed inside a market courier pouch that should never have come near a reliquary.
background.reasonInCity: She refuses to leave until she knows who moved the reliquary and why the chapel is suddenly full of frightened lies.

currentSituation.overview: Raina is triangulating chapel whispers, market gossip, and dock rumors before the cult can erase the witness trail.
currentSituation.socialStatus: Useful runner with a reputation for asking the wrong questions
currentSituation.currentProblem: She needs proof that the reliquary route is real before the remaining witnesses disappear.

goals.shortTerm: Keep the stolen reliquary clue active at the table.
goals.midTerm: Identify the courier route that carried the relic out of the chapel.
goals.longTerm: Break the harbor rite network before the city gets pulled fully into it.

spotlight.gmNotes: Use Raina when the table needs an aggressive clue engine and a reason to revisit frightened witnesses.

relationshipNotes.label: Brother Carrow
relationshipNotes.note: He keeps helping Raina, but only if she moves faster than the people watching the chapel.

specialCapabilities: Tracks repeated ritual symbols across separate scenes
Can turn dock gossip into concrete leads quickly

campaignFitSummary: Raina makes the stolen reliquary, the ruined chapel, and the market pressure all point at the same playable story line.
notes: Strong first-session reference when the GM wants one character to anchor the clue web.

relations.note: Needs his chapel access and witness memory before the story closes around him.
This clue thread is the cleanest route into her spotlight.
